summary: This inference notebook demonstrates a memory-efficient pipeline for generating competition submissions using pre-trained vision models. It loads a DINOv3 backbone with a custom Mamba-based fusion block, processes paired left/right image crops, and runs inference on test data. The author shows how to ensemble predictions from multiple folds with custom weights and apply target-specific post-processing adjustments before creating the final submission. Readers should learn how to manage GPU memory during multi-model inference, implement deterministic image splitting for test data, and apply lightweight post-processing to refine regression outputs.
competition_flow: Loads test CSV and images, processes them into resized left/right crops, runs inference through weighted ensemble of two DINOv3-based models, applies target-specific scaling, and exports a formatted submission CSV.
data_reading: pd.read_csv loads train.csv and test.csv from /kaggle/input/csiro-biomass
Test images are loaded via PIL.Image.open and converted to RGB
data_processing: Images are deterministically split into left and right halves
Resized to 512x512 and normalized with ImageNet mean/std
TTA transforms (horizontal/vertical flips, rotation) are defined but disabled via USE_TTA=False
models: BiomassModel (custom DINOv3 + LocalMambaBlock fusion + multi-head regression)
ensembling: Averages predictions from two weighted models (fold 1 weighted at 0.75, fold 3 at 1.25), then applies target-specific scaling adjustments (e.g., 0.8x for Dry_Clover_g, conditional scaling for Dry_Dead_g) before submission.
insights: Loading models sequentially and clearing CUDA cache prevents OOM errors during multi-fold inference.
Deterministic image splitting into left/right crops preserves spatial context without requiring complex data augmentation.
Target-specific post-processing scaling can effectively calibrate regression outputs for submission without retraining.
